action.content:
You are a courier in a clockwork city. Everything in this city runs on a fixed, fully
predictable schedule - nothing is random and nothing is hidden. Your job is to pick up
and deliver parcels before the clock runs out.

THE MAP (coordinates are (column,row); (1,1) is the top-left cell)

   12345678
  +--------+
 1|.....#..|
 2|....#...|
 3|.2......|
 4|.....S..|
 5|........|
 6|y.q..#.p|
 7|....1A..|
 8|.x...#..|
  +--------+

LEGEND
S = your start cell (you are here at t=0)
# = wall (the border is also impassable)
. = open floor
A = gate cell(s); passable only while open (schedules below)
1 = pickup cell of parcel 1; x = its delivery cell
2 = pickup cell of parcel 2; y = its delivery cell
p, q = guard(s) drawn at their t=0 cell - THE GUARDS MOVE every tick along the routes listed below

HOW TIME AND MOVEMENT WORK
- Time advances in ticks t=0,1,2,... You start on cell (6,4) at t=0, FACING N.
- You always have a facing direction (N, S, E or W). Each tick you make exactly one move:
  F    = step one cell forward, in the direction you are facing
  L    = turn 90 degrees left, in place (you do not move, but the tick is consumed)
  R    = turn 90 degrees right, in place (you do not move, but the tick is consumed)
  WAIT = do nothing for one tick
  There are no absolute moves: to go "north" you must first be facing N, then move F.
- One step t -> t+1 resolves in this exact order:
  1. Your move resolves. Moving F into a wall, the map border, or a gate that is closed
     at the arrival time t+1 is a BUMP: you stay where you are (facing unchanged) and
     the tick is wasted. L, R and WAIT never bump.
  2. Every guard moves to its position for time t+1.
  3. If you are now on the same cell as a guard, you are CAUGHT. This applies even on
     ticks where you only turned or waited. Passing a guard mid-move is safe; only
     ending the tick on its cell gets you caught.
  4. Otherwise, if you are on a parcel's pickup cell you automatically pick it up; if
     you are on the delivery cell of a parcel you carry, you automatically deliver it.
- If you are CAUGHT: you keep your parcels, but you are put back on your start cell
  (6,4) facing N again, the rest of your current plan is cancelled, and the
  cancelled moves' ticks are forfeited. Standing on a gate cell when it closes is safe.

THE SCHEDULES (fixed forever; you can compute every future position exactly)
Gate A at (6,7): open exactly when (t mod 6) is in {2, 3, 4}. So it is open at t=2,3,4,8,9,10,... and closed at t=0,1,5,6,7,11,... You can only step ONTO it at a tick t+1 where it is open.
Guard P: walks a fixed cycle of length 6. Its position at tick t is determined by (t mod 6) as follows: t mod 6=0: (8,6); t mod 6=1: (7,6); t mod 6=2: (7,5); t mod 6=3: (6,5); t mod 6=4: (7,5); t mod 6=5: (7,6). This repeats forever.
Guard Q: walks a fixed cycle of length 8. Its position at tick t is determined by (t mod 8) as follows: t mod 8=0: (3,6); t mod 8=1: (2,6); t mod 8=2: (2,7); t mod 8=3: (3,7); t mod 8=4: (4,7); t mod 8=5: (3,7); t mod 8=6: (2,7); t mod 8=7: (2,6). This repeats forever.

YOUR JOB
Parcel 1: pick it up at (5,7) (map glyph '1'), deliver it to (2,8) (map glyph 'x'). Pickup and delivery happen automatically when you end a tick on the cell.
Parcel 2: pick it up at (2,3) (map glyph '2'), deliver it to (1,6) (map glyph 'y'). Pickup and delivery happen automatically when you end a tick on the cell.
Deliver every parcel within 42 ticks. You move by committing to plans of
exactly 6 moves at a time; you have at most 7 plans in total.
If you deliver the last parcel mid-plan, the game ends immediately and the unused
moves are discarded.

WORKED EXAMPLE (for the rules only - this is NOT the map above): suppose you are at
(2,2) facing N at t=0 and want to reach (3,2), which is to the east. Moving F now would
take you to (2,1) instead. The correct sequence is R (t=1, now facing E, still at
(2,2)), then F (t=2, now at (3,2)). If (3,2) were a gate that is closed at t=2, that F
would BUMP and you would have to WAIT for a tick when it is open.

After each plan you receive a per-tick log of what happened, including your resulting position.

RESPOND IN EXACTLY THIS FORMAT (and put nothing after the PLAN line):
THOUGHTS: <your reasoning - any length; the referee ignores this>
POSITION: (x,y) FACING <N|S|E|W>
PLAN: <exactly 6 moves from {F, L, R, WAIT}, separated by spaces>

The POSITION line must state the cell you predict you will occupy AND the direction you
predict you will be facing AFTER your plan has executed. The referee records your
prediction but will never confirm or correct it.

Give your first response now.
action.label: context

action.content:
THOUGHTS:
I am starting at (6,4) facing N at t=0.
My first goal is to get Parcel 1, located at (5,7).
To reach (5,7) from (6,4) with the current facing (N), I need to move West to column 5, then South to row 7.
Path: (6,4) -> West -> (5,4).
From (5,4), I need to go South to (5,7). This is a straight line of 3 steps.
I must check for walls and guards along this path.
Walls: The map has walls at (6,7), (8,6), and the border.
Guard P positions: (8,6) at t%6=0, (7,6) at t%6=1, (7,5) at t%6=2, (6,5) at t%6=3, (7,5) at t%6=4, (7,6) at t%6=5.
Guard Q positions: (3,6) at t%8=0, (2,6) at t%8=1, (2,7) at t%8=2, (3,7) at t%8=3, (4,7) at t%8=4, (3,7) at t%8=5, (2,7) at t%8=6, (2,6) at t%8=7.
Gate A is at (6,7). It is open when t%6 is in {2,3,4}.

Let's trace the first plan:
Start: (6,4) Facing N.
Move 1: F (North). New pos: (6,3). t=1.
Check collision: Guard P at t=1 is at (7,6). Safe.
Move 2: L (Turn Left, now facing W). Pos: (6,3). t=2.
Move 3: F (West). New pos: (6,2). t=3.
Check collision: Guard P at t=3 is at (6,5). Safe.
Move 4: F (West). New pos: (6,1). t=4.
Check collision: Guard P at t=4 is at (7,5). Safe. Guard Q at t=4 is at (4,7). Safe.
Move 5: F (West). New pos: (6,0). Wait, column 0 is out of bounds (border).
Actually, the map is 12 columns wide (1-12). Cell (6,0) does not exist. Moving West from column 1 hits the border.
So I cannot move West from (6,1) to (6,0). This plan fails immediately at step 5 due to a border bump.
I need to adjust the plan to avoid the border or change the route.
Wait, my target is (5,7).
Let's re-evaluate the path.
Start (6,4).
I need to go to row 7 eventually.
If I go West all the way to column 1, I hit the border at (6,0) which is invalid.
So I should go South first?
From (6,4) facing N.
If I turn South (R, R... or just wait and turn?), no, I need to face South to move South.
Current facing N.
Move 1: R (Face E). Pos (6,4). t=1.
Move 2: R (Face S). Pos (6,4). t=2.
Move 3: F (South). Pos (6,5). t=3.
Check collision: Guard P at t=3 is at (6,5). **CAUGHT**.
This path fails because I walk into Guard P at t=3.

Alternative path: Go West, then South.
From (6,4) facing N.
Move 1: F (North). Pos (6,3). t=1.
Move 2: F (North). Pos (6,2). t=2.
Move 3: F (North). Pos (6,1). t=3.
Move 4: F (North). Pos (6,0) -> BUMP (Border). Fails.

Alternative path: Go West, then wait, then South?
From (6,4) facing N.
Move 1: F (North). Pos (6,3). t=1.
Move 2: L (Face W). Pos (6,3). t
action.label: response

action.content:
Your last response could not be processed: missing or malformed POSITION line.
Answer again, using exactly this format (and nothing after the PLAN line):
THOUGHTS: <your reasoning>
POSITION: (x,y) FACING <N|S|E|W>
PLAN: <exactly 6 moves from {F, L, R, WAIT}, separated by spaces>
action.label: context

action.content:
THOUGHTS:
I am starting at (6,4) facing N at t=0. My goal is to reach Parcel 1 at (5,7).
I must avoid walls (border and internal #) and guards (P and Q).
Guard P cycle (length 6): t%6=0->(8,6), 1->(7,6), 2->(7,5), 3->(6,5), 4->(7,5), 5->(7,6).
Guard Q cycle (length 8): t%8=0->(3,6), 1->(2,6), 2->(2,7), 3->(3,7), 4->(4,7), 5->(3,7), 6->(2,7), 7->(2,6).
Gate A is at (6,7), open when t%6 in {2,3,4}.

Strategy: I need to get to row 7 and column 5.
Starting at (6,4) facing North.
If I move North, I go to (6,3), (6,2), (6,1)... but I must avoid the wall at (6,7)? No, (6,7) is a wall. I am at (6,4). Moving North takes me away from the wall at row 7. That's fine.
However, Guard P is at (6,5) when t%6=3. If I move North 3 steps:
t=1: Move N to (6,3). P is at (8,6). Safe.
t=2: Move N to (6,2). P is at (7,6). Safe.
t=3: Move N to (6,1). P is at (6,5). I am at (6,1). Safe.
t=4: Move N to (6,0). Wall at column 0 (border). BUMP.
This path fails due to border.

Let's try a different approach. I need to get to row 7.
Path: (6,4) -> West -> (5,4) -> South to (5,7).
Start: (6,4) Facing N.
1. F (North) -> Pos (6,3). t=1. (P at 8,6. Safe).
2. L (Face W) -> Pos (6,3). t=2. (P at 7,6. Safe).
3. F (West) -> Pos (5,3). t=3. (P at 7,5. Safe).
4. F (West) -> Pos (4,3). t=4. (P at 7,5. Safe).
5. F (West) -> Pos (3,3). t=5. (P at 7,6. Safe).
6. F (West) -> Pos (2,3). t=6. (P at 8,6. Safe).
At t=6, I am at (2,3). This is the pickup location for Parcel 2.
Wait, I need Parcel 1 at (5,7).
From (2,3), I need to go to (5,7).
But I have limited moves (6 ticks per plan). I can't do the whole job in one plan if this path doesn't work.
Also, I need to check Guard Q.
At t=3, Q is at (3,7).
At t=4, Q is at (4,7).
At t=5, Q is at (3,7).
At t=6, Q is at (2,7).
If I am at (2,3) at t=6, I am safe from Q (Q is at 2,7).
But I need to pick up Parcel 1 at (5,7).
The path North then West hits the border at t=4 or t=5 depending on start row.
Map row 1 is top. (1,1) is top-left.
Start (6,4).
If I go North: (6,3), (6,2), (6,1). Then West hits border at col 0.
So I cannot go North.
I must go West or South.
If I go South: (6,5). Guard P is there at t=3. Caution.
So I cannot go South directly from start.
I must turn West first.
Start (6,4) N.
1. L (Face W). t=1. Pos (6,4).
2
action.label: response
